moves.name: Destabilisation
moves.definition: Remove a load-bearing premise without replacing the framework

moves.name: Inversion
moves.definition: Preserve the architecture, reverse the causal base

moves.name: Absorption
moves.definition: Incorporate a prior framework as a special case

moves.name: Rejection
moves.definition: Refuse a foundational premise from outside the framework

moves.name: Extension
moves.definition: Apply an existing framework to new terrain

moves.name: Reduction
moves.definition: Collapse complex motivation to a single mechanism

moves.name: Foreclosure
moves.definition: Build a structure that makes certain subsequent moves unavailable
